creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
I can confirm some of the problems on our 11.0-CURRENT and 10.-STABLE
boxes where I built the port x11/nvidia-driver from ports automatically
from those lines in /etc/src.conf:

/etc/src.conf:
PORTS_MODULES=
PORTS_MODULES+=         x11/nvidia-driver
PORTS_MODULES+=         emulators/virtualbox-ose-kmod

(emulators/virtualbox-ose-kmod builds fine).

This automatic build via "make buildworld buildkernel"  or
"make kernel" stops at

[...]
WRKDIRPREFIX=/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/THOR make -B clean all 
===>  Cleaning for nvidia-driver-331.38 *** [all] Stopped -- signal 22

Another observation among this stop is that I'm now asked three times
the options-box of the box: two times when the build starts, a third
time when the port is about to be installed and the terminal output
starts with 

[...]
===>  Staging for nvidia-driver-331.38
===>   Generating temporary packing list
===> src (install)
install -o root -g wheel -m 555
nvidia.ko /usr/ports/x11/nvidia-driver/work/stage/boot/modules
[...]

Performing a "portmaster x11/nvidia-driver" works fine without the
SIGNAL 22 stop mentioned above, but there is still a "three times"
request for selecting the options - it doesn't solve the problem
performing a "make distclean ; make rmconfig" (as it does in some cases
for other ports as I experienced).

As David mentioned above, on FreeBSD 9.2-STABLE the method I use
mentioned with buildworld/buildkernel/kernel works fine (also with
CLANG and driver nvidia-driver-331.38).

Oliver
